questions.prompt: What does Stripe Tax do — and NOT do?

choices.text: Files all your taxes for you.

DOES: auto-detect customer tax location, calculate correct rates per jurisdiction + product tax code, add tax to invoices, track liability, generate reports. DOES NOT: register you in jurisdictions, determine your nexus / obligation (your accountant does), file your taxes (you still file). The tool handles calculation; you're responsible for registration and filing.

questions.explanation: Stripe Tax is calculation automation, not a full compliance solution. It replaces error-prone manual tax tables but you still register in jurisdictions, decide where you have nexus, and file on schedule. Pair with an accountant.

questions.prompt: Your team sells SaaS to EU consumers. You just crossed the €10K cross-border threshold. What's the right response?

choices.text: Register for EU OSS (One Stop Shop) in your home country. Enable Stripe Tax with OSS configured. Stripe collects the correct VAT per EU country based on customer location; you file once via OSS instead of per-country. Above the threshold, charging VAT is mandatory for cross-border B2C.

questions.explanation: EU cross-border B2C above €10K triggers VAT obligation. OSS (One Stop Shop) consolidates the filing: register once in your home country; collect proper VAT per destination country; file all EU in one OSS return. Stripe Tax handles the per-charge calculation; you handle the OSS registration and quarterly filings.
